senses.definition: made to conform to a general rule or standard; made canonical or officially recognized as standard.
examples: The manuscript was canonicalized to align with the universally accepted version.
The data was canonicalized to ensure consistency across the database.

senses.definition: adds or converts something to a canonical form or standard; makes something conform to a recognized rule or norm.
examples: The program canonicalizes input data before processing.
Researchers canonicalize results to compare across studies.

senses.definition: the process of converting something to a canonical form or standard.
examples: The algorithm is currently canonicalizing the dataset.
They are canonicalizing the metadata for interoperability.

senses.definition: in a way that conforms to a canon or established standard; according to the canon.
examples: The document is canonically organized by section.
Historically, the text is canonically cited in scholarly works.

senses.definition: the state or quality of being canonical or conforming to accepted standards.
examples: The canonicalness of the text was confirmed by several experts.
They debated the canonicalness of the new interpretation.

senses.definition: items that are canonical; official standards or the canonical set within a domain.
examples: The canonicals of the library include the core historical documents.
They archived the canonicals for preservation.

senses.definition: to appoint or declare something as canonical; to recognize as belonging to the canon.
examples: The theologian canonicates certain writings as authoritative.
The manuscript was canonicated after review.

senses.definition: entities or persons that can be or are canonicated; candidates for canonization or inclusion in the canon.
examples: The canonicates of the tradition were carefully reviewed.
Several canonicates emerged during the debate.

senses.definition: plural of canonicus; in some contexts refers to a group of canons or canonical scholars in Latin-used terms.
examples: The canoni of the medieval church were gathered.
The canonici contributed to the scholarly canon.

senses.definition: plural of canonicity; the quality of being canonical or the degree to which something conforms to the canon.
examples: Different canonicities are recognized across traditions.
The canonicities of various versions were compared.

senses.definition: the quality of being canonical; the state of belonging to a recognized canon.
examples: The canonicity of the work is debated among scholars.
Maintaining canonicity is important for the tradition.

senses.definition: the body of canonical writings or the canons collectively; canonical laws or rules.
examples: The canonics of the church were codified.
Scholars study the canonics to understand doctrine.

senses.definition: the act of declaring someone or something to be part of the canon; the process of becoming canonical.
examples: The canonisation of the saint took place after careful review.
Canonisation is a formal recognition of canonicity.

senses.definition: plural of canonisation; multiple acts or instances of declaring canons or saints canonical.
examples: The canonisations over the centuries reflect changing beliefs.
Several petites canons underwent canonisations.

senses.definition: to declare something to be canonical; to recognize as part of the canon.
examples: They will canonise the new text after verification.
The scholar proposed to canonise the fragment as authoritative.

senses.definition: declared canonical; recognized as part of the official canon.
examples: The saint was canonised in the 12th century.
The manuscript was canonised after review.

senses.definition: a person who canonises; someone who declares something canonical.
examples: The canoniser reviewed the manuscript before approval.
As a canoniser, she helps determine authoritative texts.

senses.definition: plural of canoniser; multiple people who declare something canonical.
examples: The canonisers agreed on the saint's status.
Different canonisers have different criteria.

senses.definition: declares something to be canonical; recognizes as part of the canon.
examples: The body canonises new works each decade.
Experts canonise the text as authoritative.

senses.definition: the act of declaring something canonical; the process of becoming canonical.
examples: The church is canonising several saints this year.
They are canonising the manuscript after review.

senses.definition: a person who studies or writes about canons or canon law; an expert on the canon.
examples: The canonist explained the rules governing canonicity.
A renowned canonist spoke at the conference.

senses.definition: relating to canon law or canons; conforming to canonical rules.
examples: A canonistic interpretation was offered.
They discussed canonistic procedures.

senses.definition: pertaining to canons or canon law; consisting of canons; lawful according to the canon.
examples: Canonistical rules define church governance.
He studied canonistical disciplines.

senses.definition: relating to canons or canon law; of or relating to canonical procedures.
examples: Canonistical matters were debated in the council.
They followed canonistical guidelines.

senses.definition: plural of canonist; scholars or jurists who study or apply canon law.
examples: Canonists argued for a reform of rules.
The canonists contributed to legal theory.

senses.definition: an entity that causes or effects canonization; one who canonizes.
examples: The canonizant approved the saint's biography.
As a canonizant, he endorsed the text.
